title: S-Rout typed-payload construction contract
description: Specifies construction of an advertised payload tuple P_{i->j} from the current branch state, with entries declared in V and a unique consumer CTj in the interlock graph. The record operationalizes the S-Rout payload-construction lemma schema in the structural exhaustion framework (branch_closure_methodology_extended.tex, "Lemma schemas").

schema.description: Required contract discriminator; it must be the literal `S-Rout`.

payloadId.description: Required nonempty stable identifier of the residual payload; the same identifier must be used by the consumer's S-Trig record.

producerTacticId.description: Required nonempty identifier of the producing CT tactic or branch-tree node that constructs this payload after verifying the producer-side branch-state extension.

consumerTacticId.description: Required nonempty identifier of the unique downstream CT tactic that accepts responsibility for this residual and whose S-Trig consumer signature the tuple must satisfy.

tupleEntries.description: Required nonempty ordered payload tuple P_{i->j}. It must carry every object the consumer needs, such as the current residual state, declared vocabulary entries, interface data, ledger/budget records, exact types, certificate handles, or defect accounts, and no untyped placeholder.

items.description: One typed component of the payload tuple, in consumer-signature order.

name.description: Required nonempty symbol or stable reference for the component as recorded in the producer state.

sort.description: Required nonempty mathematical type expected at this tuple position, including relevant interface or admissibility refinements.

role.description: Required lifecycle role from the fixed vocabulary; a routed tuple entry normally has role `payload`, while a carried proof object may be `certificate` and carried state may be `preserved`.

definitionRef.description: Optional locator for the S-Def record, vocabulary entry, or framework definition that gives the component its type and meaning.

proofRef.description: Optional locator proving that the produced value satisfies its declared sort and residual conditions.

vocabularyVerified.description: Required literal `true`, certifying that every tuple component is already defined in the recorded vocabulary and has the declared sort.

uniqueConsumerVerified.description: Required literal `true`, certifying that the interlock/proof-flow graph assigns this payload identifier to exactly the declared consumer rather than leaving routing ambiguous.

evidence.description: Required nonempty list of sources validating payload construction, component typing, and the producer-to-consumer edge.

items.description: One evidence record supporting the route.

kind.description: Required evidence category from the fixed evidence-kind alphabet.

reference.description: Required nonempty locator for the branch constructor, transition, proof-flow diagram, dependency-table row, or typing proof.

hash.description: Optional integrity hash for a version-sensitive routing table, generated graph, or formal artifact.

notes.description: Optional note identifying the tuple entries and consumer-signature obligations established by this source.
